combat_history.event: Jinggangshan deployed to Philippines for humanitarian assistance, carrying helicopters and relief supplies
combat_history.significance: First major international deployment demonstrating China's growing humanitarian intervention capabilities

combat_history.event: Jinggangshan participated in evacuation of Chinese nationals from Yemen civil war
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated long-range deployment capability and non-combatant evacuation operations

combat_history.event: Multiple Type 071s supported establishment of China's first overseas naval base
combat_history.significance: Key logistics support for China's first permanent overseas military installation

combat_history.event: Several vessels used for quarantine and medical support during pandemic response
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated flexibility for non-traditional security missions

modernization.description: Shift focus from Type 071 production to larger Type 075 amphibious assault ships with dedicated aviation capability
modernization.impact: Type 071 now serves as secondary amphibious platform while Type 075 takes primary assault role

modernization.description: Retrofit of improved radar systems and communication equipment on existing hulls
modernization.impact: Enhanced situational awareness and coordination with other PLAN units

modernization.description: Full operational integration with indigenously produced air-cushion landing craft

vulnerabilities.description: Limited air defense capability with only short-range HQ-10 and Type 730 CIWS systems
vulnerabilities.context: Vulnerable to sustained air attack without escort vessel protection in contested environments
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Typically operates with Type 052D destroyers providing area air defense

vulnerabilities.description: Less sophisticated C4I systems compared to US or NATO amphibious vessels
vulnerabilities.context: May struggle to coordinate complex multi-domain operations in high-threat environments
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Gradual electronics upgrades and integration with broader PLAN networks

vulnerabilities.description: Limited overseas maintenance capability restricts sustained far-seas operations
vulnerabilities.context: Dependent on Chinese naval bases for major maintenance, limiting global deployment flexibility
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Development of Djibouti base and pursuit of additional overseas facilities

vulnerabilities.description: No organic ASW capability beyond basic hull-mounted sonar
vulnerabilities.context: Highly vulnerable to submarine attack without dedicated ASW escorts
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Relies on escort vessels and maritime patrol aircraft for ASW protection

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Amphibious ready group with marine battalion, supported by destroyer escort
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: 1-2 Type 071s with Type 052D destroyer and Type 054A frigate escorts
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: Higher readiness rates than earlier PLAN vessels but still learning amphibious operations doctrine

peer_comparisons.key_differences: US vessels have superior C4I and self-defense systems but similar capacity. Type 071 built faster and cheaper but less sophisticated
peer_comparisons.video_angle: East vs West amphibious doctrine: quantity vs quality in naval power projection

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Similar size and role but UK vessels have better electronics and crew training. Type 071 has larger fleet size
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Traditional naval power vs rising naval power: comparing amphibious capabilities

peer_comparisons.key_differences: French design emphasizes aviation while Type 071 focuses on surface assault. Mistral has larger aircraft capacity
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Different amphibious warfare philosophies: helicopter assault vs beach landing

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Japanese vessels smaller and focused on defensive operations. Type 071 designed for power projection
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Constitutional constraints vs expansionist ambitions in East Asian naval development

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Russian vessels much smaller and less capable, reflecting different strategic priorities and industrial limitations
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Russia-China naval cooperation: complementary capabilities or competition?

doctrine.role: Enabling rapid amphibious assault and sustained logistics support for joint operations within the first island chain, specifically designed to project PLA ground forces across the Taiwan Strait under contested conditions.
doctrine.employment: Operates as the centerpiece of amphibious ready groups alongside Type 052D destroyers and Type 054A frigates, with Type 075 LHDs providing complementary helicopter assault capability. Primary mission profiles include cross-strait assault operations, amphibious demonstrations in the South China Sea, and humanitarian assistance/disaster relief missions that double as power projection exercises. Command relationships typically place these vessels under South Sea Fleet or East Sea Fleet operational control, with embarked marine battalions maintaining independent command structures until landing operations commence.
doctrine.design_philosophy: Prioritized well deck capacity and vehicle storage over aviation facilities, reflecting PLA emphasis on heavy equipment delivery rather than helicopter-centric assault. Designers sacrificed speed and self-defense capabilities for maximum cargo capacity and shallow-water operations, accepting vulnerability to submarine and air attack in exchange for superior logistics throughput during the critical initial assault phase.
doctrine.threat_context: Originally designed for operations in a relatively permissive Taiwan Strait environment with limited U.S. intervention, assuming air superiority could be achieved by land-based aircraft. The threat environment has significantly hardened with increased U.S.-Taiwan cooperation, advanced anti-ship missiles, and submarine proliferation, making these vessels increasingly vulnerable without substantial escort protection.
doctrine.comparison_frame: Compare primarily on well deck capacity, vehicle throughput, and shallow-water capability rather than self-defense systems or aviation facilities. Speed comparisons are less relevant as all modern LPDs accept 20-25 knot performance as adequate, with cargo capacity and operational flexibility being the decisive factors for amphibious doctrine.
